items.title: Upgrade for Data Services Encrypted Extracts
items.description: As part of a security infrastructure upgrade, GnuPG (gpg2) is being upgraded from version 2.2 to 2.4 with the 1H 2026 production release. As an administrator, you must ensure your decryption systems support AEAD mode or provide an updated PGP public key without AEAD preferences before September 30, 2026, to maintain compatibility with encrypted Data Services extracts. If your PGP public key includes AEAD preferences, files encrypted after the upgrade will use AEAD mode. This may cause interoperability issues if your decryption systems run older versions of GnuPG or don't support AEAD mode, even though your public key advertises these preferences.
items.plainEnglish: Significant new capability - could change how you work. As part of a security infrastructure upgrade, GnuPG (gpg2) is being upgraded from version 2 Review config steps and test in sandbox first.

items.title: Deprecation of Managing Proxy Access Through Employee Data Import or User OData V2 API
items.description: We’re deprecating the ability to create or maintain proxy relationships through employee data imports or the User OData V2 API. Starting with the 2H 2026 release, the <PROXY> field in the user data file only displays proxy assignments. Importing the file with new <PROXY> values won't actually create, update, or delete proxy assignments. Additionally, you can still use the User OData V2 API to search for proxy assignments. However, creating, updating, or deleting proxy assignments through this API is no longer supported. You can continue to use Proxy Management and Proxy Import to create proxy assignments. A new REST API is available in 1H 2026 release for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting proxy assignments for user management. Detailed API documentation, including request and response definitions, is available in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.
items.lifecycle:
